activities_projects.title: Mapping Shaana's Journey
activities_projects.objective: Identify the geographical directions and states visited in the story
steps: Read the postcards written by Shaana.
Mark the following places on your map: Kashmir (North), Arunachal Pradesh (East), Gujarat (West), and Goa (Southwest).
Draw a line connecting the dots to show the path of her travel.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students visualize India's geography and the four cardinal directions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct marking of locations and directions on the map (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Postcard from a Friend
activities_projects.objective: Understand the format and purpose of a postcard
steps: Look at the way Shaana writes her postcards (Date, Place, Message).
Write a 3-sentence postcard to a friend about a place you want to visit.
Include one sensory detail (e.g., 'The snow was cold' or 'The air smelled like sea salt').
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice short-form descriptive writing and letter formatting.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct postcard format and use of descriptive language (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Climate Chameleon
activities_projects.objective: Identify how weather changes across different regions of India
steps: List three different places Shaana visited (e.g., Kashmir, Gujarat, Sundarbans).
Write one word to describe the weather in each place based on the text (e.g., Freezing, Hot, Humid).
Note down what Shaana wore or did in each climate.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize the climatic diversity of India.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of climatic descriptions for each region (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Animal Sightseeing
activities_projects.objective: Recall the wildlife encountered in various directions
steps: Which animal did Shaana hope to see in Arunachal Pradesh? (Red Panda).
What did she see in the Sundarbans? (Crocodiles and a snake).
Draw a small picture of one animal mentioned in the story.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students associate specific wildlife with different Indian landscapes.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct matching of animals to regions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: Directional Words
activities_projects.objective: Master terms related to geography and travel
steps: Find the words 'Glacier', 'Wetlands', 'Plateau', and 'Desert' in the text.
Write a simple definition for each.
Use 'Desert' in a sentence about Shaana's trip to Gujarat.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students build their vocabulary of geographical landforms.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct definitions and grammatical usage (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Snoring Bear Mystery
steps: Read the postcard from Himachal Pradesh.
Explain the funny mistake Shaana made: What did she think was a bear growling?
Why does this make the story more entertaining?
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify wit and lighthearted moments in a travelogue.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining the humorous situation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Transport Tally
activities_projects.objective: Identify different modes of travel used by Shaana
steps: List all the ways Shaana and her family traveled (e.g., Train, Car, Boat, Raft).
Which mode of transport took them across the Pamban Bridge?
Write which one you think would be the most exciting and why.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify various methods of transportation in India.
activities_projects.evaluation: Completion of the list and personal reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Color the Culture
activities_projects.objective: Recognize cultural elements like clothing and food
steps: In Gujarat, Shaana mentions the clothes are 'pretty and colourful'. Draw a colorful outfit she might have seen.
Look at the 'Let us discuss' section and list one famous food from your own state.
Share your drawing and food item with a classmate.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students appreciate cultural diversity within India.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to cultural themes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Facing the Tunnels
activities_projects.objective: Understand character growth and overcoming fear
steps: In the Goa postcard, how did Shaana's feelings about tunnels change?
Write down the phrase she uses to show she isn't afraid anymore.
Describe a time you were afraid of something but later felt comfortable.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze emotional shifts and personal development in characters.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of character change and personal reflection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Missing Me' Question
activities_projects.objective: Identify the social aspect of travel and sharing
steps: Shaana often asks her friends if they miss her. Why do you think she does this?
Discuss: If you went on a long trip, who would you send a postcard to first?
Practice saying 'Hello' in a language from a different part of India (e.g., Namaste, Vanakkam).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of maintaining connections while traveling.
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation in discussion and effort in learning greetings (10 marks)
